title: Postman の 422 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article provides a comprehensive and accurate evaluation of 422 (Unprocessable Entity) errors in Postman, covering common causes such as Content-Type mismatches, file attachment issues in collection runners, and data type inconsistencies, particularly in the context of FastAPI and GitHub APIs.

All important factual claims are well-supported by multiple reliable sources, including official documentation (Postman, Pydantic, GitHub), relevant RFCs, and highly-regarded community resources like Stack Overflow and Postman's own blog.
The explanations of error message components (e.g., 'loc', 'msg' in FastAPI errors, 'Resource', 'Field', 'Code' in GitHub errors) are precise and helpful for debugging.
The article's suggested solutions and troubleshooting steps are practical and align with industry best practices.

sources.title: HTTP 422 Error: How to Diagnose and Fix Unprocessable Entity Responses - Postman Blog
sources.claim: When a 422 (Unprocessable Entity) error occurs in Postman, it indicates that the HTTP request syntax is correct but the server cannot process the content of the request body. This is due to semantic issues such as incorrect request data format, Content-Type mismatch, or validation failure. It also lists common causes of 422 errors including missing required fields, incorrect data types, invalid formats, constraint violations, and incorrect Content-Type headers.

sources.title: What is HTTP 422 Error? (Unprocessable Entity) - Scrapfly Blog
sources.claim: HTTP error 422 Unprocessable Entity occurs when the server understands the request but finds that the content is syntactically correct, yet semantically invalid. Common causes include missing required fields, invalid data formats, or values that don't meet server validation rules.

sources.title: 400 Bad Request vs 422 Unprocessable Entity - Beeceptor
sources.claim: The HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity status code means that while the server was able to interpret the request sent, it is still not able to process it due to some logical error. It differentiates 422 from 400 by stating that 422 is for logical errors (validation failures) where syntax is correct, unlike 400 for syntax errors. Major causes include failing validation constraints and business rule violations.

sources.title: Understanding the 422 Unprocessable Entity Status Code | by AHMET TUNÇ - Medium
sources.claim: The 422 Unprocessable Entity status code indicates that the server understands the content type of the request, and the syntax of the request is correct, but the server cannot process the contained instructions. This status code is typically used in RESTful APIs, particularly when dealing with user input that fails validation.

sources.title: 422 Unprocessable Entity Explained - KeyCDN Support
sources.claim: The 422 (Unprocessable Entity) status code means the server understands the content type of the request entity... and the syntax of the request entity is correct... but was unable to process the contained instructions. This status was introduced in RFC 4918 and is geared toward HTTP extensions for WebDAV.

sources.title: 400 vs 422 response to POST of data - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: The 422 (Unprocessable Entity) status code means the server understands the content type of the request entity... and the syntax of the request entity is correct... but was unable to process the contained instructions. An example given is 'Content-Type: application/xml when request body is JSON'.

sources.title: Strict Mode - Pydantic
sources.claim: Pydantic operates in 'lax' mode by default, coercing compatible types, but in 'strict' mode, no type coercion occurs and values must match the exact type specified, useful for APIs and data integrity.

sources.title: Pydantic HTTP Exceptions: Handling Validation Errors in FastAPI - Orchestra
sources.claim: Pydantic is used in FastAPI to define data models for automatic request data validation. When a client sends data that doesn't conform to the model's structure, FastAPI automatically returns a 422 Unprocessable Entity error detailing the validation errors.

sources.title: Error: Cache Service Responded with 422 - Postman Blog
sources.claim: HTTP 422 errors occur when your request is syntactically correct but contains invalid data. Common causes include missing required fields, invalid data types, data format violations, and constraint violations. Type mismatches occur when you send data in a format the API doesn't expect, such as sending strings where numbers are required. It also advises checking Content-Type headers.

sources.title: Types/strict types | Pydantic Docs
sources.claim: Pydantic provides 'strict types' (e.g., StrictInt, StrictFloat, StrictStr) that only pass validation when the validated value is of the respective type, preventing coercion from compatible types.

sources.title: Types - Pydantic documentation (en)
sources.claim: Pydantic can coerce data into expected types, but 'strict mode' and 'Strict Types' disable this coercion, enforcing exact type matching.

sources.title: Strict mode | Pydantic Docs
sources.claim: By default, Pydantic attempts to coerce values, but 'strict mode' can be enabled per-model, per-field, or per-validation-call to error instead of coercing data, requiring exact type matching.

sources.title: GitHub API - 422: Unprocessable Entity: Review comments is invalid and Review threads is invalid · community · Discussion #42272 · GitHub
sources.claim: The HTTP 422 error in the GitHub REST API means 'Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.' This typically occurs when the parameters provided in the request are invalid or do not meet the requirements of the API endpoint. Troubleshooting steps include reviewing documentation, checking unique constraints, and referring to the message property for details.

sources.title: Postman Request Body: JSON, Form-data, URL-encoded & Binary Explained - YouTube
sources.claim: Explains different Postman body types (raw JSON, form-data, x-www-form-urlencoded) and the importance of Content-Type header matching the body content. It describes raw JSON as the 'go-to for structured data in modern APIs' and shows how Postman automatically adjusts the Content-Type header.

sources.title: Send parameters and body data with API requests in Postman
sources.claim: Postman allows sending raw body data, and when JSON is selected as the format, it enables syntax highlighting and appends relevant headers. It states that Postman automatically attaches the correct Content-Type header for form-data and urlencoded types, and for raw mode, it sets a header based on the selected type.

sources.title: Rename 422 from "Unprocessable Entity" to "Unprocessable Content" · Issue #664 · hyperium/http - GitHub
sources.claim: RFC 9110 (June 2022) defines 422 as "Unprocessable Content," indicating that the server understands the content type and syntax but was unable to process the contained instructions due to semantic errors.

sources.title: Getting 422 Unprocessable Entity response from GitHub Search API · community · Discussion #139773
sources.claim: The 422 Unprocessable Entity error from GitHub's Search API typically occurs due to issues with query syntax or permissions, such as an invalid 'q' query parameter or insufficient API token permissions.

sources.title: Troubleshooting the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: If a GitHub API request cannot be processed, a 422 Unprocessable Entity response and a 'Validation Failed' error message may be received. The response body includes an 'errors' property with a 'code' property to diagnose the problem.

sources.title: POSTMAN Error 422 - Unprocessable Entity, with GitHub API. What is it and how do solve it?
sources.claim: A Stack Overflow discussion mentions Content-Type mismatches (e.g., 'Changing the Content-type from text/plain to application/json in the header fixed it for me') as a cause for 422 errors with GitHub API, and also mentions issues related to invalid instructions, permissions, and outdated payloads.

sources.title: FastAPI Request Body Validation - ApX Machine Learning
sources.claim: FastAPI integrates with Pydantic models for automatic request data validation. When data doesn't conform to the model's structure, FastAPI automatically returns a JSON response with HTTP status code 422 Unprocessable Entity, detailing the validation errors with 'loc', 'msg', and 'type' properties, for example, 'value is not a valid float' or 'field required'. It also describes how FastAPI handles query vs body parameter confusion.

sources.title: FastAPI Server Errors And Logs — Take Back Control | by Roy Pasternak | Medium
sources.claim: FastAPI has built-in exception handlers for validation errors, returning a 422 status code with details about the error. It's important to check API server logs for detailed validation errors during a 422 error, and it suggests improving logging for more verbose output of client input data for debugging.

sources.title: Upload files for shared requests and cloud runs - Postman Docs
sources.claim: Postman saves file paths relative to the local working directory when attaching a file. However, for shared requests in a workspace or requests sent from a monitor/scheduled collection run (which run in the Postman cloud), the local file isn't shared or available, requiring others to have a copy or the file to be uploaded to the Postman team.

sources.title: Using JSON request body | Working with GraphQL | Postman API Network
sources.claim: Many APIs, including GraphQL, expect JSON bodies. For GraphQL, the request body is a JSON object containing a 'query' key, and Postman users should select the 'raw' type with 'JSON (application/json)' format in the Body tab.

sources.title: Postman raw JSON body Content-Type
sources.claim: Postman automatically sets the Content-Type header to application/json when 'raw' and 'JSON' are selected for the request body.

sources.title: 422 Unprocessable Content Error - Help Hub - Postman Community
sources.claim: In Postman, when the Content-Type header is 'application/json', the body should be 'raw' with 'JSON' selected. The content type and the body format must match for the API to process it correctly.

sources.title: 422 error. : r/FastAPI - Reddit
sources.claim: A 422 error in FastAPI typically means the data is not meeting what the server expects. This can be caused by Content-Type issues, such as multipart/form-data setup, or the data not being a valid JSON or dictionary.

sources.title: POST request to FastAPI endpoint produces error 422: "value is not a valid dict" | Sentry
sources.claim: A FastAPI endpoint can return a 422 error with the detail 'value is not a valid dict' if the client sends a list instead of a dictionary, for example, when using 'json.loads(df_json)' where df_json was created with 'orient="records"'.

sources.title: Python: FastAPI error 422 with POST request when sending JSON data - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: A FastAPI endpoint expecting a 'body' parameter can return a 422 'Unprocessable Entity' error if the client sends data as a query parameter instead of a JSON payload.

sources.title: Public GH repo is getting 422 Validation Failed · Issue #4352 · ossf/scorecard - GitHub
sources.claim: The GitHub Search API can return a '422 Validation Failed' error with details like '{Resource:Search Field:q Code:invalid Message:The listed users and repositories cannot be searched...}' when the query parameter 'q' is invalid.

sources.title: Handling Errors - FastAPI
sources.claim: FastAPI's RequestValidationError contains the 'loc' property, which provides information about where the validation error occurred within the request body.

sources.title: Fix FastAPI 422 Unprocessable Entity: 5 Causes and Fixes — DebugAI Blog
sources.claim: FastAPI 422 errors mean the request body does not match the Pydantic model definition, with common causes including missing fields, wrong types, nested models, and confusion between query and body parameters. It also specifically states that if Content-Type is not 'application/json' or is 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded', the body is not parsed as JSON and fields appear missing.

sources.title: File-uploads as part of a Collection run - Help Hub - Postman Community
sources.claim: In Postman, when running a collection with file uploads, if the files are not in the working directory or specified with correct relative paths, the upload may fail, even if single requests on the UI succeed.

sources.title: Set working directory - Postman Docs
sources.claim: Postman saves file paths relative to the working directory for form-data or binary file uploads. Storing files in the working directory ensures requests in shared collections work consistently.

citation_mismatches.claimed: GitHubのStack Overflowでの報告では、422エラーの原因が検索クエリの不正さに限定されるとされていますが
citation_mismatches.actual: The Stack Overflow discussion presents multiple causes for 422 errors with the GitHub API, including Content-Type mismatches, invalid instructions, permissions issues, and outdated payloads, not solely invalid search queries. The accepted answer explicitly mentions fixing a 422 error by changing `Content-Type` from `text/plain` to `application/json`.

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.

Optional improvement: Revise the 'Editor's Note' section to accurately reflect the content of the first Stack Overflow URL. The current claim that the 422 error causes in that post are 'limited to invalid search queries' is incorrect, as the accepted answer and other responses mention Content-Type mismatches and other invalid instructions for various GitHub API operations.
Optional improvement: Add links to official documentation or primary sources for key claims.
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
